---
format: revealjs
---


## Today's Agenda {background-image="Images/background-worldmap4.png" .center}

```{r}
# background-size="1920px 1080px"
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(kableExtra)
```

<br>

**III. Why is it so Hard to Cooperate with Other Countries?**

- Review outlines and write the paper

<br>

<br>

::: r-stack
Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2026)
:::

::: notes
Prep for Class

1. Check Canvas submissions

<br>

**SLIDE**: Prompt for Paper 2

:::



## Paper 2 {background-image="Images/background-worldmap4.png" .center}

Make an argument that one of the IR theories / models we've studied in class so far this term is **the most useful explanation of the event you explored in Paper 1**. 

- Options: Neorealism, Offensive Realism, Liberal Institutionalism, Economic Liberalism, and the Bargaining Model of War

Your paper must include a counter-argument that applies and evaluates a second theory to the case.

- Your counter-argument may not be from the same paradigm as the main argument

::: notes

**Questions on the prompt for the second paper?**

<br>

**SLIDE**: For today everyone submitted an outline of their second paper

:::



## For Today: Paper 2 Outline {background-image="Images/background-worldmap4.png" .smaller}

**Intro**

- What is the question?
- Why is the question important?
- Thesis statement: Theory X is most useful because...

**Premise 1: Theory 1 is most useful because... (with evidence)**

**Premise 2: Theory 1 is most useful because... (with evidence)**

**Premise 3: Theory 1 is most useful because... (with evidence)**

**Counter-Argument: Theory 2**

- What are the strengths of this explanation?
- What are the weaknesses of this explanation?
- Why is it not ultimately more useful than Theory 1?

::: notes

**Everybody ready to go with this? Outline in hand?**

<br>

Ok, time to get some feedback

- I want you to go see and give feedback on ALL of the outlines in class today!

- You will benefit from seeing our theories applied to many different events, AND

- You will benefit from making sure your arguments make sense to lots of different readers

<br>

**Questions?**

- Go!

<br>

Now that you've heard a BUNCH of arguments and gotten feedback on yours it's time to revise.

- Reflect on the feedback, revise your outline and start writing!

- Writing workshop time!

<br>

**SLIDE**: Next steps

:::



##  {background-image="Images/background-worldmap4.png" .center}

::: {.r-fit-text}
**Paper 2 is due Friday, March 20th**

<br>

**Pre-Class Assignment**

- Submit a **COMPLETED** rough draft

- Essay form (not bullets)

- With in-text citations and a bibliography

:::

::: notes

The paper is due the Friday after spring Break

- Before that Friday class you must submit a COMPLETED rough draft of that paper

- *Read slide*

<br>

**Questions?**

:::
